kaos_elements.name: Safety Goal
kaos_elements.description: Safety objective for Bookkeeping: Users must be able to generate financial reports such as balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): Users must be able to generate financial reports such as balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): The system must ensure double-entry bookkeeping principles are followed and maintain an audit trail of all transactions
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): The system must support multiple currencies and handle currency conversion
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): A bookkeeping system must record financial transactions including income and expenses
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): The system must support multiple accounts and categories
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): Users must be able to generate financial reports such as balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

steps.analysis_text: The bookkeeping system requirements have a clear alignment between safety and efficiency goals, focusing on crucial functionalities such as financial reporting, double-entry principles, and support for multiple accounts and currencies. However, the validation statuses of all elements remain pending, indicating no final assessments have been conducted yet.
steps.analysis: The bookkeeping system requirements have a clear alignment between safety and efficiency goals, focusing on crucial functionalities such as financial reporting, double-entry principles, and support for multiple accounts and currencies. However, the validation statuses of all elements remain pending, indicating no final assessments have been conducted yet.
steps.feedback: It is recommended to prioritize the validation of safety requirements as they directly impact user trust and system integrity. Concurrently, addressing efficiency requirements will enhance user experience and performance. Collaboration between SafetyAgent and EfficiencyAgent is crucial for resolving any discrepancies in the requirements.

steps.analysis_text: The bookkeeping system requirements align well with the defined responsibility and safety goals, ensuring both accountability and user safety. However, there is a need for clarity in how the responsibilities and safety measures interact, particularly regarding double-entry bookkeeping and reporting functionalities.
steps.analysis: The bookkeeping system requirements align well with the defined responsibility and safety goals, ensuring both accountability and user safety. However, there is a need for clarity in how the responsibilities and safety measures interact, particularly regarding double-entry bookkeeping and reporting functionalities.
steps.feedback: Ensure that the requirements for double-entry bookkeeping are explicitly linked to both responsibility and safety goals to maintain consistency and clarity in the system's design.
